section1.title: What is a performance audit?
section1.p1: A performance audit is a comprehensive check of your website or application: load speed, SEO factors, code quality, security, and accessibility.
section1.p2: Without an audit, optimization is guesswork. We pinpoint exactly what is slowing your project down and directly affecting your Google rankings and conversions.
section1.p3: You receive a concrete action plan — not vague advice.

section2.title: When do you need an audit?
section2.lead: Your site needs an audit if:
items: Pages take longer than 3 seconds to load
Google PageSpeed shows low scores
High bounce rate
Search rankings are dropping for no obvious reason
Users complain about slow performance
Things got slower after recent updates
You are planning to scale or launch ad campaigns

section3.title: What we check
items: Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID / INP, CLS)
Load speed on mobile and desktop
Image and media optimization
Caching, CDN, and resource compression
Code quality and dependencies (bundle size, dead code)
SEO factors: meta tags, structure, robots.txt, sitemap
Security: HTTPS, headers, vulnerabilities
Accessibility (a11y) and HTML semantics

section4.title: What you get
items: Detailed technical report with scores
Prioritized issue list (critical / important / nice-to-have)
Step-by-step instructions for fixing each issue
Before-and-after metrics comparison after implementing fixes
Forecast for improved search rankings and conversion

section5.title: How the audit works
items: Collecting information about your project and goals
Automated scanning (Lighthouse, PageSpeed Insights, GTmetrix)
Manual analysis of code, architecture, and configuration
Preparing the report with prioritized recommendations
Results walkthrough: online meeting or call
section5.footer: Timeline: 2 to 5 business days depending on project size.

auditFaq.q: How quickly can you complete the audit?
auditFaq.a: An express audit takes 1-2 business days; a full audit takes 3 to 5 days. Rush options are available if needed.

auditFaq.q: What will the final report look like?
auditFaq.a: A document with scores for each section, a prioritized issue table (critical, important, nice-to-have), and step-by-step recommendations. You will know exactly what to do next.

auditFaq.q: Do you also implement the fixes?
auditFaq.a: Yes. After the audit you can order a full implementation of the recommendations from us — we will fix everything for you.

auditFaq.q: Do you need access to the code?
auditFaq.a: A surface-level audit only requires the site URL. A deep code analysis requires access to the repository.

auditFaq.q: Does this work for mobile apps?
auditFaq.a: Yes. We audit both websites and mobile applications (React Native, Flutter, native iOS/Android).

auditFaq.q: What are Core Web Vitals and why do they matter?
auditFaq.a: Core Web Vitals are Google's metrics (load speed, interactivity, visual stability). They have been a direct search ranking factor since 2021.

auditFaq.q: How much will performance improve after the fixes?
auditFaq.a: On average clients see a PageSpeed Score increase of 20–50 points and load times improve 1.5–3×.

auditFaq.q: How much does an audit cost?
auditFaq.a: Cost depends on site size and depth of analysis. Use our calculator below to get an estimate.

express.label: Express audit
pros: Key metrics and top issues covered

pros: In-depth analysis of all system layers
